behavior_summary: Provides simple gameplay behavior for a candle item: a short description when examined, a toggleable state (likely lit/unlit) when used, and an animation/process spawn to drive visual or timed behavior.
CANDLE::look (U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::look) responds to the 'U8P_CANDLE::look' event by asking the item to 'bark' the literal string "candle ", directing that message to the requesting process/player.

CANDLE::use (U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::use) reads the item's frame, inspects the low two bits, and toggles them: if low bits == 0 it increments the frame by 1 (turns on); otherwise it clears the low two bits (turns off). The new frame is written back via Item::setFrame.
CANDLE::anim (U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::anim) spawns a separate METHOD::0610 process (spawn) using the candle process pointer — used to run candle animation or timed behavior outside the current event.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::look: linear flow — prepare process context, push pid and literal string, call Item::bark, consume return and suspend/return.
U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::use: branch on (Item::getFrame() & 0x03) == 0: true -> setFrame(frame + 1); false -> setFrame(frame & 0xFC). This encodes a binary toggle using the frame's low two bits.
U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::anim: spawns an external METHOD::0610 process and then returns; spawn is used instead of inline animation logic, isolating timed/visual behavior.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the frame low two bits: appears to represent lit/unlit or a small multi-state (inferred) but not explicitly known (confidence: medium).
Precise behavior and parameters of METHOD::0610 (spawn) invoked by U8P_CANDLE::CANDLE::anim (likely animation/timer but the method's implementation is external) (confidence: medium).

Whether CANDLE::use is triggered by a specific use verb, a proximity trigger, or inventory action in-engine (the event number is inferred as 1/use; trigger semantics are not explicit) (confidence: low).
Any global or quest flags influenced by these handlers — none observed in canonical data, but game logic elsewhere might inspect the candle frame (confidence: medium).

main_function.name: U8P_CANDLE::look
main_function.purpose: When an actor/process examines the candle, emit the short description 'candle ' via Item::bark so the player sees the item name/description.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_CANDLE::look) — triggered when the player/actor inspects or 'looks at' the candle

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLE::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_CANDLE::look' (examine) event: prepare context and call Item::bark with the string "candle ".

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLE::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' event: read item frame and toggle low two bits to switch state (likely lit/unlit).

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLE::anim
function_map.role: Spawn a separate METHOD::0610 process to run candle animation or timed behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the item's short description text to the requesting process/player using the literal string "candle ".
Read the current frame/index/state of the candle so the handler can decide how to toggle it.
Write back the new frame/index after toggling the low two bits (apply the on/off state).
Spawn a separate process/method to run candle animation or timed behavior; decouples animation from the event handler.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides simple gameplay behavior for a candle item: a short description when examined, a toggleable state (likely lit/unlit) when used, and an animation/process spawn to drive visual or timed behavior.
